title: fixed json decoding bug in daemon thread
body: fixed bug in daemon thread that is triggered when trying to convert an empty message_json to dict. Still raising the JSONDecodeError in case decoding fails for any other reason

step_plan.summary: Catch JSONDecodeError in the receive daemon loop and ignore the error when the received message is empty or whitespace, while still re-raising JSONDecodeError for non-empty invalid JSON. Add JSONDecodeError to the module imports.

steps.operation: Handle empty websocket message JSON by catching JSONDecodeError and ignoring empty/whitespace payloads; add JSONDecodeError to imports
steps.reason: The receive daemon currently calls json.loads(message_json) with no handling for json.decoder.JSONDecodeError. When message_json is empty or only whitespace, json.loads raises JSONDecodeError and the daemon thread crashes. Wrapping the loads call in a try/except JSONDecodeError and ignoring the exception only when the raw message is empty/whitespace prevents the thread from stopping while preserving error propagation for genuinely malformed non-empty JSON.
steps.side_effects: Behavioral: The recv daemon will no longer raise an exception (and crash) for empty/whitespace messages — it will silently ignore them. For non-empty but invalid JSON payloads, the JSONDecodeError will still be re-raised, preserving existing error signaling. Tests: add/update unit/integration tests simulating empty messages and malformed JSON; existing tests that expect JSONDecodeError on empty messages will need adjustment. Dependents: Any code that relied on the daemon thread terminating on empty messages will observe different behavior (daemon continues). API: no public API changes; internal import modified to include JSONDecodeError (may affect import ordering/linting). Operational: ensure existing websocket exception handling (timeouts, connection errors) remains unchanged and that any logging remains intact so diagnostics for ignored empty messages are still possible.
